Scoring Framework — 10 Filters + Red Flags

100-point system: 10 dimensions × 10 points each. Filter 11 is a binary reject gate.

Verdict Thresholds

GEM ≥ 75
STRONG ≥ 65
ACCEPTABLE ≥ 55
WATCHLIST ≥ 40
REJECT ≥ 0

Filter Definitions

Filter 1: Valuation (10/10)

Sector-aware PE/PB scoring. PEG, EV/EBITDA, P/S ratios. Graham Number and company-specific DCF intrinsic value. Reverse DCF to check what growth the market implies. Margin of safety = how much below intrinsic value.

Sources: Damodaran, Graham, Mauboussin
  • PE vs sector norms (cheap/avg/expensive)
  • PB vs sector norms
  • PEG ratio (< 1 ideal)
  • EV/EBITDA (< 8 ideal)
  • P/S ratio (< 2 ideal)
  • Margin of Safety (Graham Number)
  • Reverse DCF implied growth vs actual growth

Filter 2: Profitability (10/10)

How efficiently does the company convert capital into profit? ROE > 15% = good. ROCE > 20% = Buffett territory. Owner Earnings = Net Income + D&A - Maintenance CapEx.

Sources: Buffett, Greenblatt, Mukherjea
  • ROE (min 12%, excellent 25%)
  • ROCE (min 15%, excellent 25%)
  • ROA (min 5%, excellent 12%)
  • Operating Margin (min 12%, excellent 25%)
  • Net Margin (min 8%, excellent 20%)
  • Owner Earnings yield

Filter 3: Growth Quality (10/10)

Revenue CAGR shows demand. Profit CAGR shows leverage. Incremental ROCE (ROIIC) = delta NOPAT / delta invested capital. A company with ROCE 20% but ROIIC 8% is running out of high-return reinvestment opportunities.

Sources: Lynch, Srinivasan, Mukherjea (ROIIC)
  • Revenue CAGR 3yr (min 10%, excellent 20%)
  • Profit CAGR 3yr (min 12%, excellent 25%)
  • Incremental ROCE / ROIIC
  • Quarterly profit direction (improving/mixed/declining)
  • Earnings consistency (YoY volatility)

Filter 4: Financial Health (10/10)

Balance sheet fortress. Altman Z-Score predicts bankruptcy within 2 years. Low debt, high interest coverage, adequate liquidity.

Sources: Altman, Graham, Marks
  • Altman Z-Score (> 2.99 safe, < 1.81 distress)
  • Debt-to-Equity (< 1.0 ideal)
  • Interest Coverage (> 3x good, > 6x excellent)
  • Current Ratio (> 1.5 good)

Filter 5: Cash Flow (10/10)

Cash is truth; earnings are opinion. FCF Conversion = FCF / NI. Must be > 80%. FCF yield = how cheap on a cash basis.

Sources: Greenblatt, Srinivasan, Buffett
  • Free Cash Flow positive (binary)
  • FCF Conversion Ratio (FCF / Net Income)
  • FCF Yield % (FCF / Market Cap)
  • OCF / Net Income ratio
  • CapEx intensity (CapEx / Revenue)

Filter 6: Business Moat (10/10)

Sustainable competitive advantage. 7 moat types. Moat TREND: widening (rising ROCE + margins) vs eroding (declining ROCE + growing revenue = price war).

Sources: Dorsey (Morningstar), Buffett, Porter
  • Number of moat types identified (0-7)
  • ROCE strength (durability proxy)
  • Operating margin strength (pricing power)
  • Revenue growth (demand signal)
  • Moat trend: widening / stable / eroding

Filter 7: Earnings Quality (10/10)

Piotroski F-Score: 9 binary tests (0-9). Top scores outperform by 7.5% annually. Accrual Ratio = (NI - OCF) / TA. High accruals = paper earnings = manipulation risk.

Sources: Piotroski (Stanford), Beneish, Mukherjea
  • Piotroski F-Score (0-9)
  • Accrual Ratio (lower is better, negative = quality)
  • OCF > Net Income (cash backs earnings)
  • Gross margin trend (improving = efficiency)

Filter 8: Institutional Backing (10/10)

Smart money validation. High institutional ownership = due diligence done. Analyst target = external valuation check.

Sources: Lynch, Institutional theory
  • Institutional holding % (20-70% ideal)
  • Insider holding % (> 10% = skin in the game)
  • Analyst recommendation (buy/hold/sell)
  • Analyst target vs current price (upside %)

Filter 9: Sector & Macro (10/10)

Structural tailwinds, disruption risk, diversification. Cyclicals get normalised PE. Capital cycle awareness.

Sources: Dalio (macro), Marathon AM (capital cycles)
  • Sector tailwinds (policy, TAM, macro)
  • Diversification (revenue mix, geographic spread)
  • Future threats (disruption, regulation, competition)
  • Company growth premium/discount vs sector
  • Cyclicality adjustment

Filter 10: Management Quality (10/10)

Quantitative proxy for management quality. Capital allocation (ROCE), shareholder returns, financial discipline, margin trend, no dilution.

Sources: Buffett, Munger, Fisher
  • ROCE consistency (capital allocation)
  • Dividend payout ratio (15-60% ideal)
  • Debt discipline (D/E low or declining)
  • Margin trend (expanding/stable/contracting)
  • Share dilution (shares outstanding trend)
  • FCF positive (cash generative)

Filter 11: Red Flags (Binary Reject) (Binary Reject)

Binary kill-switches. A single red flag = uninvestable.

Sources: Altman, Graham, Piotroski, Mukherjea
  • PE > 60 (extreme overvaluation)
  • D/E > 2.0 (overleveraged)
  • Interest Coverage < 1.5x (debt stress)
  • Profit declining 3+ consecutive quarters
  • Negative free cash flow
  • Market Cap < 500 Cr (micro-cap risk)
  • Current year net loss
  • Altman Z-Score < 1.81 (bankruptcy risk)
  • Piotroski F-Score <= 2 (worst quality)
  • Accrual Ratio > 25% (accounting fraud risk)

Coffee Can Criteria (Saurabh Mukherjea)

Buy and hold for 10 years. Eligible if: Revenue CAGR ≥ 10%, ROCE ≥ 15%, D/E ≤ 1.0.

Magic Formula Cross-Check (Joel Greenblatt)

Combined rank of Earnings Yield (EBIT/EV) and ROIC (ROCE proxy). Lower rank = better value + quality combo. Used as a sanity check, not a scoring input.
